title: lippia vernonioides
intro: Lippia vernonioides, also known as Mexican oregano or Mexican marjoram, is a plant species in the family Verbenaceae. It is a shrub-like herb native to Mexico and Central America, valued for its aromatic leaves and medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Lippia vernonioides can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving stress, and promoting immune function.
